docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Lama is a Grasshopper plugin for finite element analysis using CalculiX. It lets you model, mesh, and analyse structures with beam, shell, and solid elements — all within a parametric Grasshopper workflow.

Lama is developed by Marco Pellegrino.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Meshing – Generate tetrahedral meshes via Gmsh directly in Grasshopper
  • Materials – Isotropic, orthotropic, spring, and stiffness matrix materials
  • Elements – Tetra, hexa, and shell elements
  • Analysis – Linear static, nonlinear static, dynamic implicit, and frequency steps
  • Post-processing – Read CalculiX results and visualise deformed shapes and stresses

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
Head to Installation to set up Lama, then explore the Basics to learn how each component works.

Note

The author cannot be held responsible for the output. Results should always be validated against another finite element analysis package.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Grasshopper is a visual programming language for Rhino3D, a popular 3D modelling software. You can install Lama using the Package Manager or manually.

docs.title: Package Manager
docs.text:
1. Open Rhino3D and launch the Package Manager from the Tools menu — or click the link.

  2. Search for Lama and click Install. The Package Manager will download and install the plugin.

  3. Restart Rhino3D to activate the plugin.

Note

If you have correctly followed the steps above, you will find the Lama tab on the Grasshopper toolbar.

docs.title: Install CalculiX
docs.text:
Lama requires a CalculiX (ccx) executable to run analyses.

  1. Download CalculiX from calculix.de or use a prebuilt binary such as bConverged.
  2. Note the path to ccx.exe — you will need it in the Run Analysis component.

Note

You can set the CalculiX path once in the Run with Exe component and it will be remembered for future sessions.

docs.title: Build Input Deck
docs.text: The Build Input Deck component writes the assembled model to a CalculiX .inp file.

docs.title: Run Analysis
docs.text:
The Run with Exe component executes CalculiX on the generated input deck.

Parameter Description Input Deck Path Path to the .inp file CalculiX Path Path to the ccx executable

Note

Ensure CalculiX is installed and the path is correct before running.

docs.title: Assemble Model
docs.text: The CcxModel component assembles all parts of the structural model into a single CalculiX model ready for analysis.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Input Description Mesh / Model The meshed structural model Materials Material definitions Sections Section assignments Boundary Conditions Supports and constraints Loads Applied loads Steps Analysis steps with output requests

docs.title: Output
docs.text: A complete CalculiX model object that can be passed to the Build Input Deck or Run Analysis components.

docs.title: Fixed Support
docs.text:
The Support component applies fixed boundary conditions to selected nodes, constraining all translational and rotational degrees of freedom.

Select nodes by point location or by node set.

docs.title: Elements
docs.text: Lama supports the following CalculiX element types:

docs.title: Shell Elements
docs.text: Element Nodes Description Shell3 3 Triangular shell Shell4 4 Quadrilateral shell Shell6 6 Quadratic triangular shell Shell8 8 Quadratic quadrilateral shell

docs.title: Solid Elements
docs.text: Element Nodes Description Tetra4 4 Linear tetrahedron Tetra10 10 Quadratic tetrahedron Hexa20 20 Quadratic hexahedron (brick)

docs.title: Gravity Load
docs.text:
Applies a gravitational body force to the entire model.

Parameter Description Direction Gravity vector (e.g. 0, 0, -1) Magnitude Acceleration (e.g. 9.81)

docs.title: Nodal Load
docs.text:
Applies a concentrated force or moment at selected nodes.

Parameter Description Node Target node(s) Force Force vector (Fx, Fy, Fz)

docs.title: Materials
docs.text: Lama supports several material models for use with CalculiX.

docs.title: Isotropic Material
docs.text:
A linear elastic isotropic material defined by:

Parameter Description Young's Modulus Elastic modulus (E) Poisson's Ratio Poisson's ratio (ν) Density Mass density (ρ)

docs.title: Orthotropic Material
docs.text: An orthotropic material with independent properties in three directions.

docs.title: Spring Material
docs.text: A spring-type material for point-to-point spring elements.

docs.title: Stiffness Matrix Material
docs.text:
A general stiffness matrix material for advanced element formulations.

Note

For most structural analyses, the Isotropic Material component is sufficient.

docs.title: Meshing
docs.text: Lama integrates Gmsh for tetrahedral mesh generation.

docs.title: Tetra Mesh
docs.text: The Gmsh Tetra Mesh component generates a tetrahedral mesh from a Brep geometry.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Input Description Brep The geometry to mesh Mesh Size Target element size Mesh Order Element order (1 = linear, 2 = quadratic)

docs.title: Output
docs.text: A tetrahedral mesh that can be converted to a Lama structural model using the Tetra Mesh to Model component.

docs.title: Hex Mesh
docs.text: Hexahedral meshes can be imported and converted using the Hex Mesh to Model component.

docs.title: Read Results
docs.text: The Read Results component parses CalculiX output files (.dat, .frd) and extracts analysis results.

docs.title: Visualisation
docs.text:
The Vis Results component displays deformed shapes and stress contours in the Rhino viewport.

Results include:

  • Displacements – nodal translations
  • Stresses – element/nodal stress tensors (averaged at nodes)
  • Reaction forces – support reactions
  • Mode shapes – from frequency analysis

docs.title: Sections
docs.text: Sections define the cross-sectional properties assigned to elements.

docs.title: Beam Section
docs.text: Defines a beam cross section with geometric properties (area, moments of inertia) and an orientation vector.

docs.title: Shell Section
docs.text: Defines a shell section by its thickness.

docs.title: Solid Section
docs.text: Defines a solid section — used for 3D tetrahedral and hexahedral elements. Typically only requires a material assignment.

docs.title: Analysis Steps
docs.text: Steps define the type of analysis to perform.

docs.title: Linear Static
docs.text: A single-step linear elastic analysis. The most common step type for basic structural problems.

docs.title: Nonlinear Static
docs.text: An incremental nonlinear static analysis with load stepping and convergence control.

docs.title: Dynamic Implicit
docs.text: A time-history dynamic analysis using implicit time integration.

docs.title: Frequency
docs.text: A modal/eigenvalue analysis to extract natural frequencies and mode shapes.

docs.title: Output Requests
docs.text: Each step can include output requests for nodal and element results (displacements, stresses, reaction forces, etc.).

docs.title: Cantilever Beam
docs.text: A simple example demonstrating the basic Lama workflow.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: A cantilever beam of length 1 m, fixed at one end, with a point load at the free end.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Create a beam geometry in Rhino
  2. Mesh with the Gmsh Tetra Mesh component
  3. Assign an Isotropic Material (e.g. Steel: E = 210 GPa, ν = 0.3)
  4. Define a Solid Section
  5. Apply a Fixed Support at one end
  6. Apply a Nodal Load at the free end
  7. Add a Linear Static Step with output requests
  8. Assemble the model
  9. Build Input Deck and Run Analysis
  10. Read Results and Visualise

Note

More detailed examples with Grasshopper screenshots will be added soon.

docs.title: CalculiX
docs.text:
CalculiX is a free, open-source finite element solver developed by Guido Dhondt and Klaus Wittig.

Lama uses CalculiX as its analysis backend. The input deck format (.inp) is compatible with the CalculiX CrunchiX (ccx) solver.

docs.title: Useful links
docs.text:
• CalculiX homepage
  • CalculiX documentation (PDF)
  • bConverged prebuilt binaries
